title: CIP Header Schema
description: JSON Schema for validating YAML frontmatter headers in Cardano Improvement Proposals (CIPs)

CIP.description: The CIP number (without leading 0), or one or more '?' before being assigned

Title.description: A succinct and descriptive title. Don't use backticks (`) in titles since they disrupt formatting in other contexts. Must be less than 100 characters.

Category.description: One of the editorially accepted categories covering one area of the ecosystem

Status.description: Status of the CIP: Proposed, Active, or Inactive with a required parenthetical reason (e.g., 'Inactive (Superseded by CIP-NNNN)')

Authors.description: A list of authors' real names and email addresses (e.g. John Doe <john.doe@email.domain>)

Implementors.description: A list of implementors committed to delivering an implementation, 'N/A' when not applicable, or [] when there's currently no implementor

Discussions.description: A list of links where major technical discussions regarding this CIP happened. Must include a link to the pull request that created the CIP. Each entry must have a label followed by a colon and URL. Can be a string 'Label: URL' or a dictionary {'Label': 'URL'}.

Solution To.description: A list of CPS (Cardano Problem Statements) that this CIP addresses, if any. Omitted when not applicable. Each entry is a bare CPS reference like 'CPS-0001' (or 'CPS-0001?' if the CPS is still an open PR).

items.description: CPS reference, zero-padded to at least 4 digits (e.g., 'CPS-0001' or 'CPS-0001?')

Created.description: Date created on, in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) format
